message:
test(core): cross-module integration tests for data structure composition

Add integration tests verifying that independently-developed data
structure modules compose correctly in realistic pipeline scenarios.
These catch interface mismatches and semantic assumptions that unit
tests within individual modules cannot detect.

Test scenarios (825 lines, 4 groups):

A. Entropy-aware eviction pipeline:
   - EntropyEstimator scores pane segments by Shannon entropy
   - ScrollbackEvictor uses entropy scores to prioritize eviction order
   - Verifies that high-entropy (information-rich) segments survive
     eviction while low-entropy (repetitive) segments are reclaimed first
   - Tests interaction between EvictionConfig thresholds and
     InformationBudget constraints

B. Bloom-filter accelerated deduplication:
   - BloomFilter provides probabilistic fast-path rejection
   - ContentStore (SHA-256) provides definitive dedup decisions
   - DedupEngine orchestrates the two-tier lookup
   - Verifies false-positive handling: bloom says "maybe duplicate" but
     ContentStore correctly identifies novel content

C. Stream integrity with sharded metrics:
   - Rolling hash detects content corruption in capture streams
   - Sharded atomic counters track bytes/segments without lock contention
   - Verifies that metric increments from concurrent capture tasks
     produce consistent totals

D. Completion token orchestration:
   - CompletionTracker manages multi-step capture pipeline state machines
   - Tests step transitions (pending → active → complete/failed),
     boundary detection, and timeout handling
   - Verifies that completion tokens compose correctly when multiple
     panes have independent capture pipelines in flight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
